report:
## Summary
Cory, your performance has shown some improvement over the last few weeks, particularly with a notable increase in hours worked on Monday (15.7h) of the most recent week. However, your overall weekly totals still fall short of the goal, indicating an opportunity for further growth.

## What's Working
- Your Monday performance on 2026-05-15 was strong at 15.7 hours, showcasing your potential for high productivity early in the week. 💪
- You have consistently engaged with complex diagnostics, as seen with RO 777209, demonstrating your commitment to thorough troubleshooting.
- The engine work on RO 777231 has progressed well, with parts arriving and engine approval, indicating your ability to manage significant repairs effectively.
- Your pacing has improved slightly, with 47.8 hours pacing noted in the most recent week, suggesting potential for increased productivity moving forward.

## Where to Focus
- The total hours for the week of 2026-05-09 to 2026-05-15 were only 38.2, which is 80.4% of the goal. This indicates a need to maximize productivity on days with lower hours, such as Tuesday (2.4h) and Wednesday (3.1h). Consider planning to tackle smaller jobs or diagnostics on these days to increase your output. 📈
- You have had several weeks below 30 hours (e.g., 22.9h on 2026-05-02 to 2026-05-08). It’s crucial to aim for more consistent performance across the week to meet your goal. Focus on scheduling more jobs, especially during mid-week. 
- The aging WIP on RO 777209 is concerning, as it has been open for 12 days. Prioritize communication with parts to get updates on back-ordered items and keep the customer informed to maintain satisfaction. 
- There are several jobs awaiting assignment (e.g., RO 777590 and RO 777565) that could help boost your hours. Make it a goal to pick up at least one of these jobs this week to diversify your workload and increase your billed hours.

## Action Items for This Week
1. Follow up on parts for RO 777209 and ensure you are actively communicating with the customer about the delay.
2. Pick up at least one job from the shared queue this week to increase your billed hours and maintain a steady workflow.
3. Aim to complete the diagnostics on RO 777231 quickly to keep the momentum going and ensure timely billing.

## WIP Watchlist
- RO 777209 is aging and needs attention due to back-ordered parts. Keep pushing for updates and be proactive in communication. 
- RO 777231 is ready for the next steps; ensure you’re moving forward with the engine work this week.

Keep up the good work, Cory! Let’s aim for a strong week ahead! 🚗

## Summary
Cory, your performance has shown some improvement over the last few weeks, particularly with your productivity peaking at 33.6 hours during the week of April 4th to April 10th. However, your recent weekly totals have dropped, averaging just 26.4 hours last week, which is significantly below our goal of 47.5 hours.

## What's Working
- You achieved a notable 33.6 hours the week of April 4th to April 10th, demonstrating your capability to meet demands when fully engaged.
- Your work on April 11th, where you billed 11.8 hours, shows you can have highly productive days when focused.
- You have effectively managed to complete several complex repairs, such as the engine issue on RO 777108, indicating strong technical skills.
- Your ability to handle high-priority ROs, including the engine knocking and battery issues, shows you’re trusted with critical tasks that require immediate attention.

## Where to Focus
- Your total hours have consistently been below the goal, with only 26.4 hours last week (April 25th to May 1st). This is concerning as it impacts overall department efficiency. Aim to increase your daily billable hours, especially on Mondays and Fridays where you had lower outputs.
- The RO 777108 (engine work) is 32 days old. It’s important to prioritize this and push for completion, as aging ROs can impact customer satisfaction and workflow.
- The RO 777209 (battery issue) requires immediate follow-up. Completing this quickly can help you regain momentum and improve your weekly performance metrics.
- You have several high-priority jobs in the shared queue, particularly RO 777092 (timing issue) and RO 777158 (A/C issue). Picking one of these up could help boost your hours and demonstrate your proactive approach to problem-solving.

## Action Items for This Week
1. Prioritize completing RO 777108 (engine) to reduce aging ROs and focus on getting parts installed.
2. Follow up on RO 777209 (battery) to ensure it’s resolved quickly, allowing you to move on to the next tasks.
3. Select and take on one of the high-priority jobs from the shared queue, such as RO 777092 (timing issue), to increase your billable hours this week.

## WIP Watchlist
- Focus on RO 777108 (engine) as it is aging and requires timely completion. Also, keep an eye on the high-priority ROs in the shared queue to maximize your productivity this week.
